outcome_contract.why: Drift between near-duplicate screens is compounding silently; attribution data is already missing from apartment bookings, and visual inconsistencies are live. Fixing now prevents further compounding before the next feature layer touches these screens.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Both booking screens use the canonical analytics pattern (attribution present on all handoffs); visual tokens are consistent; double-room has component-level tests matching apartment coverage.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Fix 6 class tokens across 3 elements in apartment only
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same fix applies via hook output
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Fix applied via base component render
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Visual token fixes scoped to `ApartmentBookContent` only; dark mode borders on date card, rate card, WhatsApp CTA

engineering_coverage.applicable: No state changes; `isValidRange` and pax state unchanged
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Same — pax state would need prop-level configuration

engineering_coverage.applicable: Apartment migrated to `trackThenNavigate` + attribution fields; beacon transport preserved; null guard required
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Attribution fields present on all apartment handoffs post-migration; field parity with double-room verified

engineering_coverage.applicable: Apartment test updated; new double-room test suite added
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Hook unit tests required in addition to component tests
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Base component tests required in addition to room-specific tests
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: 3 targeted test changes: update apartment analytics test, add double-room analytics + URL tests

engineering_coverage.applicable: GA4 event payload: must preserve all existing fields; new attribution fields added
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Field parity acceptance criterion required in IMPLEMENT task

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Single commit; rollback affects both components simultaneously
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Preferred: independent rollback per task type

stage_payload.decision_summary: `ApartmentBookContent.tsx` and `DoubleRoomBookContent.tsx` have accumulated silent drift on three independent fronts: (1) analytics — apartment sends no attribution data; (2) visual tokens — NR card, saving badge, and dark mode borders are inconsistent; (3) test coverage — double-room has zero tests. The decision is whether to fix these divergences in-place or first create shared infrastructure.
goals: Migrate apartment analytics to the canonical `trackThenNavigate` + attribution pattern (highest impact).
Correct visual token drift (NR button, badge text, dark mode borders on 3 elements).
Add double-room component-level tests to close the coverage gap.
non_goals: Unifying pax handling, rate codes, or room-specific copy.
Adding a CI-level duplicate screen detector.
constraints: Existing GA4 event fields must be preserved — only the call pattern changes.

`fireHandoffToEngine` must remain in `ga4-events.ts` (it has no other runtime callers after migration, but its mock is referenced in `apartment-booking-url-matrix.test.tsx`; becomes dead code after migration — follow-up cleanup is out of scope for this plan).
assumptions: `trackThenNavigate` + `readAttribution` is the canonical analytics pattern going forward.
Visual token drift is bugs, not intentional design differences (NR=accent vs NR=primary).
`isLongStay` is apartment-specific; correct to be absent from double-room.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Attribution data absent from apartment = all apartment bookings unattributable in GA4
Change must be trivially revertible — no shared infrastructure change
Fewer tasks = less risk of build-time decision points
Double-room gap leaves analytics and URL logic untested
NR card token inconsistency is user-visible on two live pages

options_considered.Description: Directly fix each confirmed drift divergence in the individual components. Three independent tasks: analytics migration, visual token correction, double-room tests. No shared abstraction.
options_considered.Upside: Minimal blast radius. Each change rollbackable individually. Pattern already proven in double-room.
options_considered.Downside: Both components remain structurally separate — future drift is still possible without a shared base.
options_considered.Key risks: Null guard omitted in analytics migration; NR token design intent ambiguity.

options_considered.Option: B — Shared hook extraction, then fix
options_considered.Description: Extract shared booking state + analytics logic into a custom hook (`useBookingHandoff`), then migrate both components to use it, fixing drift as part of the extraction.
options_considered.Upside: Single source of truth for analytics logic going forward.
options_considered.Downside: Larger surface area — touching both components and adding a new hook in the same change. Increases risk of regression in apartment while fixing drift. Disproportionate effort for 3 isolated divergences.
options_considered.Key risks: Migration of two components simultaneously multiplies rollback risk. Shared hook adds an abstraction layer not yet proven necessary.

options_considered.Downside: Intentional differences (pax range 2–3, rate code map, FitCheck, i18n namespaces, long-stay flag) require significant prop surface. Risk of new abstraction coupling two room types that may legitimately diverge further.
options_considered.Key risks: Over-engineering for current scope; risk of hiding intentional differences inside a combined prop API.

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A — fix each confirmed drift divergence directly in `ApartmentBookContent.tsx` with no shared infrastructure changes.
chosen_approach.Option B and C introduce shared abstract: the intentional differences (pax range, rate codes, FitCheck, namespaces) are real and would require significant prop engineering to accommodate cleanly.
chosen_approach.Null guard required (`attributionFields: {}`) — this is the double-room pattern and must be replicated.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (shared hook extraction)** — disproportionate scope for 3 isolated divergences. Increases regression risk by touching both components in the same change. The shared abstraction would need to handle apartment-specific pax state and FitCheck rendering, making it a non-trivial hook. Deferred to a future fact-find if drift continues to compound.

**Option C (full shared base)** — over-engineering. Intentional differences (pax 2-3 vs fixed 2, rate code maps differ, FitCheck presence, i18n namespaces, long-stay flag) are substantial. A shared base component would need to accommodate all these via props or render slots, adding complexity without a clear benefit at current scale. Not warranted until a third private-room booking screen emerges.

sequencing_constraints: All three tasks are independently runnable. No cross-task dependency.
Analytics task should be first (highest business impact, most complex).

process_topology.note: None: no material process topology change
